title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts empty TLS path fields
summary: Oracle MySQL TLS path fields accepted empty strings.
observed: An empty ssl_ca, ssl_cert, or ssl_key value could pass early validation and fail later in the database driver.
expected: TLS path fields must be non-empty strings without padding or control characters.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for TLS path validation.
impact: TLS configuration mistakes are delayed and harder to diagnose.
delivery_semantics: The fix is startup validation and does not change write or ACK behavior.
security: Failing closed on malformed TLS paths helps preserve certificate verification by default.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL TLS path fields reject empty strings.
TLS certificate and key pairing rules remain enforced.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
documentation: Document strict TLS path field validation.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
